name: Panel (HoloViz Python Data App Framework)

auth.notes: No built-in auth; configure via server settings or reverse proxy

performance.notes: Bokeh server for web rendering; reactive pipeline; Param for state management

agent_readiness.notes: Python data app framework in the HoloViz ecosystem. import panel as pn. pn.extension(). slider = pn.widgets.IntSlider(start=0, end=100). @pn.depends(slider). def plot(val): return df.plot(). pn.Column(slider, plot).servable(). panel serve app.py. Reactive with Param. Works with matplotlib, Plotly, Bokeh, hvPlot, Altair, Vega. Jupyter + standalone server. More flexible than Streamlit but more complex. Less opinionated than Streamlit — supports complex multi-page apps. LLM chat widgets. WebGL support. Deployment to HuggingFace Spaces, Heroku, cloud.

gotchas: Steeper learning curve than Streamlit (Param reactive model)
Deployment less turnkey than Streamlit Cloud
HoloViz ecosystem lock-in for hvPlot/HoloViews integration
Server state management requires understanding Param library
best_when: Complex multi-page data apps requiring more flexibility than Streamlit
Jupyter-first workflows serving apps from notebooks
When Streamlit's reactive model is too limiting
avoid_when: Simple data apps — Streamlit faster to build
Teams unfamiliar with the Param/HoloViz ecosystem
When Plotly Dash's richer component ecosystem is needed
summary: Panel is HoloViz's Python data app framework — more flexible than Streamlit, less opinionated. pn.widgets.IntSlider() + @pn.depends(slider) reactive decorators. Works with matplotlib, Plotly, Bokeh, hvPlot, Altair. panel serve app.py for standalone server. Jupyter + standalone server deployment. Param-based reactive state management. LLM chat widgets. More complex than Streamlit but supports sophisticated multi-page apps. BSD-3 license.
